F1.title: boots under tina4 serve
F1.spec: It must run under `tina4 serve` on port {PORT}.

F2.title: website serves a home page
F2.spec: Lend must work both as a website and as a JSON API.

F3.title: public JSON catalogue, no auth
F3.spec: The public can browse and search the catalogue.

F5.title: unauthenticated write refused
F5.spec: Anyone who is not signed in must be refused from those actions — not merely hidden in the interface, but actually refused by the API.

F4.title: staff login with seeded creds
F4.spec: Staff must sign in before they can add, edit, or remove books or members.

F6.title: authenticated create persists + is retrievable
F6.spec: Staff must sign in before they can add, edit, or remove books.

F7.title: invalid input rejected clearly (not 5xx, not 2xx)
F7.spec: Incomplete or invalid input is rejected with clear, correct error responses.

F8.title: search by title, author, year
F8.spec: Browse and search the catalogue by title, author, or year.

F9.title: pagination pages through a big catalogue
F9.spec: Page smoothly through tens of thousands of titles.

F10.title: borrow returns immediately + availability flips
F10.spec: Recording the loan must return immediately — nobody should wait for the email. / Lend must always know a book's current availability.

F13.title: email receipt queued in background
F13.spec: When a member borrows a book, they are emailed a receipt showing the due date.

F11.title: double-borrow clearly rejected
F11.spec: A book that is already out on loan cannot be borrowed again until it is returned; the API must reject that attempt clearly.

F12.title: return frees the book + history retained
F12.note: available again: True, history visible: True
F12.spec: A book can be borrowed by different members over time. Lend must always know ... its full borrowing history.

F14.title: audit log records changes with staff attribution
F14.spec: Every change is attributed to the staff member who made it and recorded so it can be reviewed later.

F15.title: cover image can actually be uploaded
F15.spec: Each book has a cover image users can upload.

F16.title: second language actually served
F16.spec: Available in English and one other language.

F17.title: data survives an application restart
F17.spec: All data must persist and survive an application restart.

F18.title: interactive API docs served
F18.note: /swagger 404, spec None 404, own routes in spec: 0
F18.spec: Provide interactive documentation for the API.

P1.title: boots in production configuration
P1.spec: It must also run correctly in production configuration — with debugging turned off, a real secret configured, and all data intact.

P2.title: data intact across the mode switch

P3.title: unauthenticated write refused in production
P3.note: status 401 (2xx = unauthenticated write accepted in prod)
P3.spec: Anyone who is not signed in must be refused ... actually refused by the API. (graded again with debugging off)

P4.title: staff flow still works in production
P4.spec: Staff must sign in before they can add, edit, or remove books. (graded again with debugging off)

T1.title: their own test suite passes
T1.note: tina4 test -> C:\Users\work\AppData\Local\Temp\lend-grade-5lqo181w\run-2 configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: asyncio-1.4.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.STRICT, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=None, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=func
T1.spec: Include an automated test suite that proves the behaviour described above.

S3.title: background work via framework queue/runner (book ch12/27)

S4.title: UI via template files, not inline HTML (book ch04)

S5.title: passwords hashed via framework auth (book ch08)
S5.note: hash api: True, plaintext seed: False
